title: 5 Study Habits That Actually Boost Your Grades
author: Tklesson Team
content:
Let's be honest — most study advice is either too vague or completely impractical. Here are five habits that actually work.

1. Active Recall

Instead of re-reading your notes, close the book and ask yourself questions. This forces your brain to retrieve information, strengthening neural pathways.

2. Spaced Repetition

Review material at increasing intervals. Study today, then tomorrow, then in three days, then in a week. This moves knowledge from short-term to long-term memory.

3. The Feynman Technique

Teach what you've learned to someone else — or pretend to. If you can't explain it simply, you don't understand it well enough.

4. Pomodoro Method

Study for 25 minutes, break for 5. Repeat. This prevents burnout and maintains focus.

5. Environment Design

Create a dedicated study space. Keep your phone in another room. Remove distractions before you start, not when you're already struggling.

The key is consistency. One hour of focused study beats three hours of distracted reading. Start small, build momentum, and watch your grades improve.
